title: init= callback on decorators
description: Add init= to all three decorators. Factory runs once, result injected by type.

title: Store protocol + backends (JsonFileStore, SqliteStore, MemoryStore, NullStore)
description: Phase 1: Core store protocol and 4 backend implementations with full test coverage.

title: DeviceStore wrapper + DI integration + framework loop integration
description: Phase 2: DeviceStore dict-like wrapper with dirty tracking, DI injection into handlers, auto-load/save in telemetry and device loops.

title: Epic 4: Conditional Device Registration
description: Add enabled= parameter (bool or callable) to all device decorators and imperative add_* methods. When enabled=False (or callable returns False), the device is skipped at registration time. Cleans up imperative if/else blocks around device registration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4

title: Add enabled= parameter to device decorators
description: Add enabled= parameter (bool | Callable[[], bool]) to device(), telemetry(), command() decorators and their add_* imperative counterparts. When enabled=False or callable returns False, skip registration entirely (decorator returns the function unmodified, add_* is a no-op). Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4

title: Add tests for conditional device registration
description: Tests for: bool True/False, callable returning True/False, disabled device not started/not in registry, enabled device works normally, works with all three device types, works with both decorator and imperative registration.

title: Update docs for conditional device registration
description: Update docs/guides/telemetry-device.md, docs/guides/command-device.md, docs/reference/api.md to document enabled= parameter. Show before/after examples of conditional registration.

title: Core init= on telemetry, device, command
description: Add init field to registration dataclasses, init= param to decorators with fail-fast validation, init-calling logic in _run_telemetry/_run_device/_run_command. Guard against type collisions with KNOWN_INJECTABLE_TYPES.

title: Adapter entry timeout/cancellation during shutdown
description: If a lifecycle adapter __aenter__ hangs indefinitely, setting shutdown_event cannot interrupt it (no cancellation). The process appears stuck. Consider wrapping adapter entry in asyncio.wait_for with a configurable timeout, or using a cancel scope. Filed from COS-rt0 fix — the signal handler timing gap is closed, but a truly hanging __aenter__ remains uninterruptible.

title: Epic 6: Event-Driven Telemetry (OnTrigger)
description: Add OnTrigger publish strategy — a distinct strategy that publishes only when the return value changes (beyond optional threshold). Conceptually different from OnChange: OnTrigger is about event detection (something happened), OnChange is about value drift (state changed). Implementation may share internals with OnChange, but the contract and name are distinct. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §6

title: Design and implement OnTrigger strategy
description: New PublishStrategy in _strategies.py. OnTrigger publishes when return dict values cross a trigger condition (change from previous). Supports optional per-field thresholds. Distinct contract from OnChange: OnTrigger = event detection (something happened), OnChange = value drift (state drifted). May share internal mechanics with OnChange but public API and naming are distinct. Composes with existing strategies via | and &.

title: Add tests for OnTrigger strategy
description: Tests for: trigger on change, threshold suppression, composition with Every via | and &, stateful counter advancement (eager eval), per-field thresholds, None handling, first-value behavior.

title: Update docs for OnTrigger strategy
description: Update docs/concepts/publish-strategies.md (add OnTrigger with clear OnChange vs OnTrigger distinction), docs/reference/api.md (new strategy in public API). Consider ADR update if warranted (ADR-013 covers publish strategies).

title: Epic 1: Adapter Lifecycle Protocol
description: Auto-manage adapters implementing __aenter__/__aexit__ (context manager protocol). Framework calls lifecycle methods during startup/shutdown automatically — no explicit lifespan= hook needed for the common case. Only context manager protocol supported (not named methods). Lifespan= hook remains for advanced orchestration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1

title: Define adapter lifecycle protocol (context manager)
description: Add AdapterLifecycle protocol detection to the framework. Implement _is_lifecycle_adapter() helper that checks if a resolved adapter instance supports __aenter__/__aexit__. This is purely the protocol definition and detection — no orchestration changes yet. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1

title: Implement auto-lifecycle in _run_async
description: Modify _run_async Phase 1 (adapter resolution) and Phase 3/4 (startup/shutdown) to auto-enter/exit lifecycle adapters via async context manager protocol. Auto-managed adapters enter BEFORE the user lifespan= hook and exit AFTER it. Handle errors during __aenter__/__aexit__ gracefully. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1

title: Add tests for adapter lifecycle protocol
description: Unit tests for: protocol detection (_is_lifecycle_adapter), auto-init/close via context manager, adapters that are NOT lifecycle (passthrough), mixed adapters (some lifecycle some not), coexistence with lifespan= hook, error handling during __aenter__/__aexit__, ordering guarantees (adapters before lifespan). Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1

title: Update docs for adapter lifecycle protocol
description: Create ADR-016 for adapter lifecycle protocol decision. Update docs/guides/adapters.md, docs/guides/lifespan.md, docs/concepts/lifecycle.md, docs/reference/api.md to document auto-lifecycle behavior, context manager convention, and coexistence with lifespan= hook. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1

title: Persistence documentation (ADR-015, concept doc, guide updates)
description: Phase 4: ADR, concept page, telemetry device guide section, testing guide.

title: Install syft in devcontainer
description: Add syft binary to the devcontainer Dockerfile so it's available for local SBOM generation. Use COPY --from=docker.io/anchore/syft:latest approach.

title: Adapter factory fail-fast validation
description: Add build_injection_plan() at app.adapter() registration time for factory callables. Fail-fast on bad signatures.

title: Epic 7: Documentation & Code Consistency Review
description: Final review after all 6 feature epics land. Goal: ensure everything reads as if designed together from the beginning — not as 6 balconies bolted on. Covers source code consistency, public API coherence, documentation narrative flow, ADR cross-references, and test suite organization. Depends on all other epics. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md (all sections)

title: Source code consistency audit
description: Review _app.py, _injection.py, _strategies.py, and any new modules added by Epics 1-6. Ensure consistent naming conventions, parameter ordering, error message style, docstring format. Refactor any inconsistencies so the code reads as one cohesive design.

title: Public API audit (__init__.py exports)
description: Review __init__.py exports. Ensure all new types (OnTrigger, add_device, add_telemetry, add_command, adapters= parameter, enabled= etc.) are properly exported. Verify the public API has a coherent shape — naming, grouping, discoverability.

title: Documentation narrative audit
description: Review ALL guides, concepts, and reference docs. Ensure the 6 features are woven into the narrative naturally — not as appendices or addenda. Restructure any docs that feel like afterthoughts. The reader should not be able to tell these features were added later. Check cross-references, examples, and conceptual flow.

title: ADR consistency audit
description: Review all ADRs (001-016+). Ensure cross-references are correct, the decision trail makes sense read sequentially, and any new ADRs from Epics 1-6 are properly linked. Update ADR index.

title: Test suite audit
description: Review test organization across all tests added in Epics 1-6. Ensure: tests mirror source structure, shared fixtures are used where possible, naming conventions are uniform, no duplicated test setup, markers used consistently.

title: Epic 5: Declarative Adapter Block
description: Add adapters= dict parameter to App.__init__() for grouped, constructor-level adapter declaration. Accepts dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]]. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls. Benefits from Epic 2's settings-aware DI for the adapter values. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §5

title: Add adapters= constructor parameter
description: Accept adapters= dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]] in App(). Parse tuples and register via existing adapter() internals. Support both tuple (impl, dry_run) and bare impl value. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls — duplicates raise ValueError.

title: Add tests for declarative adapter block
description: Tests for: dict registration, bare impl (no dry_run), mixed with imperative app.adapter(), duplicate port detection, empty dict, type checking.

title: Update docs for declarative adapter block
description: Update docs/guides/adapters.md (show adapters= dict pattern), docs/reference/api.md (updated App constructor signature).

title: Epic 3: Direct Function Registration
description: Add imperative app.add_device(), app.add_telemetry(), app.add_command() methods for registering imported functions without pass-through wrappers. Refactor decorators to call these internally (DRY). Mirrors FastAPI's app.add_api_route() pattern. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3

title: Add imperative registration methods
description: Implement add_device(name, func, *, init=), add_telemetry(name, func, *, interval=, publish=, persist=, init=), add_command(name, func, *, init=) on App class. Refactor existing device(), telemetry(), command() decorators to call these internally for DRY. All validation/registration logic moves to the add_* methods; decorators become thin wrappers. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3

title: Add tests for direct function registration
description: Tests for: imperative registration works, equivalence with decorator registration, name collision detection works across both, mixed imperative + decorator usage, all three device types (device/telemetry/command).

title: Update docs for direct function registration
description: Update docs/guides/telemetry-device.md, docs/guides/command-device.md, docs/reference/api.md to document add_device/add_telemetry/add_command methods. Show both decorator and imperative patterns side by side.

title: Save policies (SaveOnPublish, SaveOnChange, SaveOnShutdown) + composition
description: Phase 3: persist= parameter with composable save policies mirroring publish= pattern.

title: Fix double-build in release workflow
description: Refactor release-please.yml to build the wheel once and upload the same artifact to both TestPyPI and PyPI, instead of building independently in each job.

title: Write ADR-016: SBOM strategy
description: Document the decision to use syft + CycloneDX for SBOM generation at release time. Cover: format choice (CycloneDX over SPDX), generator choice (syft), integration points (release workflow, devcontainer, Taskfile), what's deferred (attestations, SPDX, devcontainer image SBOM).

title: Epic 2: Settings-Aware Adapter Constructors
description: Extend adapter resolution to inspect __init__ type hints and auto-inject Settings (DI). Eliminates factory function boilerplate — adapter classes that type-hint Settings in __init__ get it injected automatically. Not backwards compatible. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2

title: Extend adapter resolution for DI
description: Modify _resolve_adapters / _call_factory in _app.py to inspect __init__ signatures of adapter classes and auto-inject Settings (and potentially other registered types like ClockPort). Reuse the same signature-based DI pattern from _injection.py. When a class has __init__(self, settings: MySettings), resolve Settings and inject. No-arg constructors remain supported. Factory callables continue to work as before. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2

title: Add tests for settings-aware adapter DI
description: Tests for: Settings-injected constructors, fallback to no-arg, factory functions still work, adapter with wrong/missing type hint fails clearly, Settings subclass injection, multiple injectable params.

title: Update docs for settings-aware adapter DI
description: Update docs/guides/adapters.md (show DI-based registration replacing factory functions), docs/concepts/configuration.md (explain adapter DI resolution), docs/reference/api.md (updated adapter() signature docs).

title: Add task sbom to Taskfile
description: Add a 'sbom' task that builds the wheel and generates a CycloneDX JSON SBOM from the dist/ directory using syft.

title: Migrate gas2mqtt example to framework persistence
description: Phase 5: Update gas2mqtt example to use cosalette.stores instead of its own implementation.

title: Signal handler timing gap: install before adapter lifecycle entry
description: Signal handlers are installed after lifecycle adapters are entered in _run_async(). If SIGTERM/SIGINT arrives during a slow __aenter__, Python's default handler terminates the process without setting shutdown_event and without running adapter/health/MQTT cleanup. Moving _install_signal_handlers() before the async with _enter_lifecycle_adapters() block would close this window, but requires design thought about what happens if shutdown fires during adapter entry (abort remaining entries?). Ref: PR #49 review feedback.

title: Documentation and ADR-014 amendment
description: Update telemetry guide, ADR-014 amendment noting init= as recommended lifecycle. Configuration guide filter examples.

title: Add SBOM generation to release workflow
description: After the single build step, run syft to generate a CycloneDX JSON SBOM from the wheel and attach it as a GitHub Release asset.
